So in terms of the Google Business Pro product that we're talking about, it, it focuses around the Google business profile which Tammy, I know you're already aware.

Um But just I guess for everybody else on the call, ultimately, it's, it's super important to have a good profile because overall it's the first thing that a renter is going to see whenever they are going to your website.
Um It establishes more social credit ability and it enables communication.
So what we do, um there's three big things that we do to help with.
That one would be daily postings on the Google business profile.
The second thing would be a product's pricing availability feed.
And the third would be that chat button like Alex was mentioning earlier.
It does um integrate with uh me lease or um your yardie A I um chat A I that you or chat IQ that you were uh talking about as well.
So in terms of how that looks, um I actually pulled up one of Drucker and Fox properties that's using us um over in Hampton, Virginia.
So the first thing here is the products pricing availability feed.
So, Tammy, whenever we showed it to you originally, we were only doing individual floor plans.
So it's a one bedroom, two bedroom, three bedroom.
Now, um since you're using Yardie um in rent cafe and we integrate with them, we can go down to the granular floor plan level data.
So um if you have like bedroom one, a bedroom one B, we'll advertise all of those as long as they're available and we'll advertise the lowest available price as well.
Um All of these, if you're using a pricing tool, it does update daily.

So if you have a pricing tool that changes it once a day or once a week, it automatically reflect on our end and then kind of taking it a step further, let's say like your floor plan, the oval didn't have any availabilities at this property.
It wouldn't reflect on here because we don't wanna drive rent or traffic that way for something that's not available.
The second big piece here is our daily postings.
So there's three things that Google looks for when it comes to postings.
One is frequency.
So we knock that out, we do a post every single day.
Um Most of the times it's going to include pictures and words, sometimes we may skip out on a picture just to make sure we can keep things different.
Um But ultimately, we hit it every single day.
The second thing they really look for is relativity.
So when it comes to relativity, every single post that we do is going to be admin or floor plan focused.
So you're not gonna see something crazy about come enjoy the cupcakes on Fourth of July in our leasing office.
Um And then the third thing would be uniqueness.

Now, what I mean by uniqueness is a lot of social media companies will either a post the same exact posts across every single one of your properties or it'll post the same posts on Google as it does on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and all your social media sites.
We have over a million different variations of posts to make sure that we're not cross duplicating.
Um So every single one of these posts you can tell are different.
Um Wording is different, pictures are mostly different.
Um And then you're not gonna see something about this property show up on a different property.
Um In terms of the chatbot, it's now fully functional on an a web browser versus just the mobile devices.
Um But somebody can click chat here.
Um And it'll ask and be able to answer nearly any question that you can pull from your website or property management software.
